CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-05-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On The Ground Of Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jan-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

DEFENDANT MARK ACKER'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On The Ground Of Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction is off calendar as moot. Dismissal of entire action, with prejudice filed on January 5, 2016.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/EG)

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2015 Continued To Feb-24-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On The Ground Of Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Nov-16-2015 Continued To Jan-04-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT MARK ACKER'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On The Ground Of Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction is continued to January 4, 2016 per the agreement of the parties. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =(302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On The Ground Of Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Nov-05-2015 Continued To Nov-16-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT MARK ACKER's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On The Ground Of Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction is continued to November 16, 2015 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than November 16, 2015 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The Court has courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers.Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/ehg
